quick_facts.label: What it is
quick_facts.value: RRB JE is a Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) competitive recruitment process for Junior Engineer posts in Indian Railways; the latest detailed JE notification also included Depot Material Superintendent (DMS) and Chemical & Metallurgical Assistant (CMA) posts.

quick_facts.label: Latest notified cycle
quick_facts.value: The latest detailed notice located is CEN 05/2025. Its online application window ran from 31 October 2025 to 30 November 2025; this is a past window, not an open application deadline.

quick_facts.label: Age context
quick_facts.value: For CEN 05/2025, the advertised age range for the JE/DMS/CMA group was 18–33 years as on 1 January 2026, with specified category-based upper-age relaxations. Age criteria can change in a future CEN.

quick_facts.label: Qualification context
quick_facts.value: Eligibility is post-specific and must be checked against the notification’s Annexure A. The 2025 technical exam-group mapping covered a three-year engineering diploma or a bachelor’s degree in Engineering/Technology in listed disciplines, including mechanical, electrical, electronics/computer-related, and civil/allied streams.

quick_facts.label: Selection stages
quick_facts.value: The notified process consists of CBT-I, CBT-II, document verification, and medical examination. CBT-I is a screening test; shortlisting for document verification is based on CBT-II merit and options.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: For CEN 05/2025, JE/DMS/CMA posts were advertised at 7th CPC Pay Level 6 with initial pay of ₹35,400. This is an advertised basic-pay figure for that CEN, not a guarantee of take-home pay; allowances and future terms may vary.

sections.heading: Purpose and scope of RRB JE
paragraphs: RRB JE is not an academic entrance examination or engineering qualification. It is a centralised recruitment route through the Railway Recruitment Boards for technical railway roles, principally Junior Engineer positions. Individual notifications can combine JE hiring with related technical posts, so applicants should read the specific Centralised Employment Notice (CEN), including its post parameters, vacancy table, medical standard, and discipline mapping.

In the latest located cycle, CEN 05/2025 advertised 2,569 combined vacancies for JE, DMS and CMA across RRBs. Vacancy figures are expressly provisional and may change according to Railway Administration requirements.
bullets: Choose the RRB carefully: the CEN permits one RRB and one common application; multiple applications can result in rejection.
Post, railway zone/production unit, and eventual allocation are governed by eligibility, preferences, merit, medical fitness, and vacancies rather than by the examination score alone.

sections.heading: Eligibility: check the exact post, discipline, age and medical standard
paragraphs: Eligibility is determined by the particular CEN rather than by the exam name alone. Under CEN 05/2025, candidates had to possess the prescribed educational, technical or professional qualification from a recognised institute or university by the application closing date; candidates awaiting final results were not eligible. The post-wise requirements appear in Annexure A, so a diploma or degree should not be assumed eligible unless its discipline is mapped to the intended post.

For the 2025 cycle, the standard age band for the JE/DMS/CMA group was 18–33 years on 1 January 2026. The notice provided upper-age relaxations for specified categories, including SC/ST, OBC-NCL, PwBD, ex-servicemen and certain railway employees. Candidates also need to meet the applicable nationality/citizenship conditions and the medical standard of the posts for which they opt.
bullets: Use the Annexure A post parameters—not coaching-site summaries—to match the diploma/degree branch to the post.

Review the required medical category before applying. Medical fitness, including vision standards for relevant categories, is assessed by Railway Administration; failing the prescribed standard does not lead to an alternative appointment.

Candidates already employed by a Central/State Government department, Railways or a PSU may apply after informing their employer, but shortlisted candidates must produce the required NOC at document verification.

sections.heading: Exam pattern and selection format
paragraphs: CEN 05/2025 specifies two objective, multiple-choice computer-based tests, followed by document verification and a medical examination. CBT-I is common to the notified posts and functions as a screening stage; its normalised score is used to shortlist candidates for CBT-II. CBT-II performance is used for shortlisting to document verification, subject to merit and the candidate’s options.

CBT-I was specified as 100 questions in 90 minutes: Mathematics (30), General Intelligence and Reasoning (25), General Awareness (15), and General Science (30). CBT-II was specified as 150 questions in 120 minutes, covering General Awareness, Physics and Chemistry, computer basics, environment/pollution control, and post-relevant technical abilities. The notice allows indicative section splits to vary in the actual paper.
bullets: Negative marking in CBT is one-third of the marks allotted to a question for each incorrect answer.
For CEN 05/2025, questions were offered in English, Hindi, and 13 listed regional Indian languages; candidates selected their medium in the application.

The latest RRB Chandigarh notice page records that CBT-I for CEN 05/2025 was held on 19, 20 and 25 February 2026, and that CBT-II had been tentatively scheduled for 2 July 2026. Always verify subsequent notices on the relevant official RRB site.

sections.heading: Application process and fee context
paragraphs: RRB applications are made online through the application link published on official RRB websites. The CEN 05/2025 procedure required candidates to create or use an RRB account, select only one RRB, complete the application accurately, upload the required live photograph/signature and documents, pay the applicable fee, and retain the submitted application details. A personal active mobile number and email address are required for OTPs and recruitment communications.

The CEN 05/2025 window is closed: applications were accepted from 31 October through 30 November 2025, with a correction window from 3 to 12 December 2025. Future recruitment dates, fees, correction rules and document requirements must be taken only from the next official CEN.
bullets: CEN 05/2025 charged ₹500 for most candidates, with ₹400 refundable after appearing in CBT-I, less applicable bank charges. It charged ₹250 for SC, ST, ex-servicemen, PwBD, female, transgender, minority and EBC candidates, refundable after appearing in CBT-I, less applicable bank charges. These were cycle-specific fees.
In the 2025 notice, Aadhaar or DigiLocker authentication was strongly advised to facilitate verification; alternative photo-ID verification could involve stricter scrutiny.
Download city intimation, e-call letters and any travel authority only from official RRB links. RRBs warn applicants against fake sites and job touts.

sections.heading: Preparation approach aligned to the official syllabus
paragraphs: Prepare in two layers. First, build speed and accuracy for the CBT-I screening syllabus: mathematics, reasoning, general awareness, and Class 10-level science. Second, devote substantial time to the CBT-II technical syllabus that matches the exam group selected through the eligible engineering discipline; CBT-II also includes general awareness, Class 10-level physics and chemistry, computer basics, and environment/pollution control.

Because incorrect answers attract one-third negative marking, preparation should include timed practice, disciplined question selection, and review of errors—not merely maximising attempts. Use the official CEN’s Annexure VII technical syllabus for the relevant civil, electrical, electronics, mechanical or CMA exam group, and use the RRB-provided practice test when available to become familiar with the CBT interface.
bullets: Create a post-specific checklist from the CEN: eligible qualification, exam group, technical syllabus, medical standard and preferred posts/zones.
Practise CBT-I as a 90-minute, 100-question screening test and CBT-II as a 120-minute, 150-question technical test.
Treat current-affairs material as time-sensitive and update it near the examination date; the official syllabus identifies General Awareness but does not prescribe a fixed current-affairs cutoff.

sections.heading: What selection can lead to
paragraphs: Candidates who clear the applicable CBT stages, document verification and medical examination may be empanelled according to merit, options and vacancy availability. The RRB recommends empanelled candidates, while the concerned Railway Administration issues the offer of appointment. Therefore, passing a CBT or appearing on a shortlist is not itself an appointment guarantee.

For the 2025 CEN, the combined JE/DMS/CMA group was advertised in Pay Level 6 with initial basic pay of ₹35,400. Actual appointment, posting, allowances, training and service conditions depend on the post, railway administration and terms prevailing at appointment. The notice states that selected candidates may undergo prescribed training and receive a stipend during that training period.
bullets: Potential JE workstreams in the notification included civil works, permanent way/bridges, track machines, electrical services, workshops, mechanical carriage-and-wagon work, diesel, and signalling/telecommunication, subject to the vacancies and post parameters notified.
Railway-zone or production-unit allocation follows candidate options, merit, medical fitness and vacancies; an initial posting does not create an automatic right to transfer to another railway.
Document verification compares original supporting documents with application claims. A mismatch or failure to meet qualification, age, category or medical requirements can cancel candidature.
